title: Incorrect `hashjoin_highcardinality` benchmark
description:
### What happens?

Hi Team,

While running the benchmark suite, I came across this unexpected behavior:

```
тЮЬ  duckdb git:(main) тЬЧ build/release/benchmark/benchmark_runner benchmark/micro/join/hashjo
in_highcardinality.benchmark 
name    run     timing
benchmark/micro/join/hashjoin_highcardinality.benchmark 1       INCORRECT
INCORRECT RESULT: Catalog Error: Column with name v2 already exists!

====================================================
================  FAILURES SUMMARY  ================
====================================================

1: benchmark/micro/join/hashjoin_highcardinality.benchmark
name    run     timing
benchmark/micro/join/hashjoin_highcardinality.benchmark 1               INCORRECT
INCORRECT RESULT: Catalog Error: Column with name v2 already exists!

```

However, running the same on terminal gives the output:

I ran this on duckdb terminal, and I see the correct output:
```
D create table t1 as select i as v1, i as v2 from range (0, 1000) t(i);
D create table t2 as select i as v1, i as v2 from range (0, 10000000) t(i);
D select t1.v2, t2.v2, count(*) from t1 inner join t2 on (t1.v1 = t2.v1) group by t1.v2, t2
.v2 order by t1.v2 limit 5;
тФМтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФмтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФмтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФР
тФВ  v2   тФВ  v2   тФВ count_star() тФВ
тФВ int64 тФВ int64 тФВ    int64     тФВ
тФЬтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФ╝тФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФ╝тФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФд
тФВ     0 тФВ     0 тФВ            1 тФВ
тФВ     1 тФВ     1 тФВ            1 тФВ
тФВ     2 тФВ     2 тФВ            1 тФВ
тФВ     3 тФВ     3 тФВ            1 тФВ
тФВ     4 тФВ     4 тФВ            1 тФВ
тФФтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФ┤тФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФ┤тФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФШ

```

### To Reproduce

1. Trigger a Benchmark build using this: `BUILD_BENCHMARK=1 BUILD_TPCH=1 make`

2. Run this command: `build/release/benchmark/benchmark_runner benchmark/micro/join/hashjo
in_highcardinality.benchmark`


### OS:

macos

### DuckDB Version:

Build from master

### DuckDB Client:

None

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Roshan Swain

### Affiliation:

Individual Contributor

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a source build

Yes

- [x] Yes, I have

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (v1 INT, v2 INT, v3 INT DEFAULT 0);
CREATE TABLE t2 (v1 INT, v2 INT, v4 VARCHAR(255), v5 FLOAT);

INSERT INTO t1 SELECT i, i, i % 2 FROM (SELECT 0 AS i UNION ALL SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT 4 UNION ALL SELECT 5 UNION ALL SELECT 6 UNION ALL SELECT 7 UNION ALL SELECT 8 UNION ALL SELECT 9 UNION ALL SELECT 10) AS range(i) CROSS JOIN (SELECT 0 AS dummy UNION ALL SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT 4 UNION ALL SELECT 5 UNION ALL SELECT 6 UNION ALL SELECT 7 UNION ALL SELECT 8 UNION ALL SELECT 9 UNION ALL SELECT 10) AS dummy LIMIT 1001;

INSERT INTO t2 SELECT i, i, CONCAT('value', i), i * 1.0 FROM (SELECT 0 AS i UNION ALL SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT 4 UNION ALL SELECT 5 UNION ALL SELECT 6 UNION ALL SELECT 7 UNION ALL SELECT 8 UNION ALL SELECT 9 UNION ALL SELECT 10) AS range(i) CROSS JOIN (SELECT 0 AS dummy UNION ALL SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT 4 UNION ALL SELECT 5 UNION ALL SELECT 6 UNION ALL SELECT 7 UNION ALL SELECT 8 UNION ALL SELECT 9 UNION ALL SELECT 10) AS dummy LIMIT 10000001;
SELECT t1.v2, t2.v2, COUNT(*) FROM t1 INNER JOIN t2 ON (t1.v1 = t2.v1) GROUP BY t1.v2, t2.v2 ORDER BY t1.v2 LIMIT 5;
key_bug_features: Column with name v2 already exists error
